rules.description: Classes decorated with @Directive must have suffix "Directive" (or custom) in their name. Note: As of v20, this is no longer recommended by the Angular Team.

rules.name: directive-selector
rules.description: Directive selectors should follow given naming rules. See more at https://angular.dev/style-guide#choosing-directive-selectors.

rules.name: no-async-lifecycle-method
rules.description: Angular Lifecycle methods should not be async. Angular does not wait for async lifecycle but the code incorrectly suggests it does.

rules.name: no-attribute-decorator
rules.description: The @Attribute decorator is used to obtain a single value for an attribute. This is a much less common use case than getting a stream of values (using @Input), so the @Attribute decorator is often mistakenly used when @Input is intended. This rule disallows the usage of @Attribute decorator entirely to prevent these mistakes.

rules.name: no-developer-preview
rules.description: Disallow using code which is marked as developer preview

rules.name: no-duplicates-in-metadata-arrays
rules.description: Ensures that metadata arrays do not contain duplicate entries.

rules.name: no-empty-lifecycle-method
rules.description: Disallows declaring empty lifecycle methods

rules.name: no-experimental
rules.description: Disallow using code which is marked as experimental

rules.name: no-forward-ref
rules.description: Disallows usage of \`${FORWARD_REF}\` references for DI

rules.name: no-implicit-take-until-destroyed
rules.description: Ensures that \`takeUntilDestroyed()\` is called with an explicit \`DestroyRef\` when used outside of an injection context

rules.name: no-input-prefix
rules.description: Ensures that input bindings, including aliases, are not named or prefixed by the configured disallowed prefixes

rules.name: no-input-rename
rules.description: Ensures that input bindings are not aliased

rules.name: no-inputs-metadata-property
rules.description: Disallows usage of the \`${METADATA_PROPERTY_NAME}\` metadata property

rules.name: no-lifecycle-call
rules.description: Disallows explicit calls to lifecycle methods

rules.name: no-output-native
rules.description: Ensures that output bindings, including aliases, are not named as standard DOM events

rules.name: no-output-on-prefix
rules.description: Ensures that output bindings, including aliases, are not named "on", nor prefixed with it. See more at ${STYLE_GUIDE_LINK}

rules.name: no-output-rename
rules.description: Ensures that output bindings are not aliased

rules.name: no-outputs-metadata-property
rules.description: Disallows usage of the \`${METADATA_PROPERTY_NAME}\` metadata property

rules.name: no-pipe-impure
rules.description: Disallows the declaration of impure pipes

rules.name: no-queries-metadata-property
rules.description: Disallows usage of the \`${METADATA_PROPERTY_NAME}\` metadata property.

rules.name: no-uncalled-signals
rules.description: Warns user about unintentionally doing logic on the signal, rather than the signal's value

rules.name: pipe-prefix
rules.description: Enforce consistent prefix for pipes.

rules.name: prefer-host-metadata-property
rules.description: Use `host` metadata property instead of `@HostBinding` and `HostListener`

rules.name: prefer-inject
rules.description: Prefer using the inject() function over constructor parameter injection

rules.name: prefer-on-push-component-change-detection
rules.description: Ensures components do not opt out of the default \`${STRATEGY_ON_PUSH}\` change detection strategy

rules.name: prefer-output-emitter-ref
rules.description: Use `OutputEmitterRef` instead of `@Output()`

rules.name: prefer-output-readonly
rules.description: Prefer to declare `@Output`, `OutputEmitterRef` and `OutputRef` as `readonly` since they are not supposed to be reassigned

rules.name: prefer-signal-model
rules.description: Use `model` instead of `input` and `output` for two-way bindings

rules.name: prefer-signals
rules.description: Use readonly signals instead of `@Input()`, `@ViewChild()` and other legacy decorators

rules.name: prefer-standalone
rules.description: Ensures Components, Directives and Pipes do not opt out of standalone.

rules.name: relative-url-prefix
rules.description: The ./ and ../ prefix is standard syntax for relative URLs; don't depend on Angular's current ability to do without that prefix.

rules.name: require-lifecycle-on-prototype
rules.description: Ensures that lifecycle methods are defined on the object's prototype instead of on an instance.

rules.name: require-localize-metadata
rules.description: Ensures that $localize tagged messages contain helpful metadata to aid with translations.

rules.name: runtime-localize
rules.description: Ensures that $localize tagged messages can use runtime-loaded translations.

rules.name: sort-keys-in-type-decorator
rules.description: Ensures that keys in type decorators (Component, Directive, NgModule, Pipe) are sorted in a consistent order

rules.name: sort-lifecycle-methods

rules.name: use-component-selector
rules.description: Component selector must be declared

rules.name: use-component-view-encapsulation

rules.name: use-injectable-provided-in
rules.description: Using the \`${METADATA_PROPERTY_NAME}\` property makes \`Injectables\` tree-shakable

rules.name: use-lifecycle-interface
rules.description: Ensures that classes implement lifecycle interfaces corresponding to the declared lifecycle methods. See more at ${STYLE_GUIDE_LINK}

rules.name: use-pipe-transform-interface
rules.description: Ensures that \`Pipes\` implement \`${PIPE_TRANSFORM}\` interface

rules.name: alt-text
rules.description: [Accessibility] Enforces alternate text for elements which require the alt, aria-label, aria-labelledby attributes.

rules.name: attributes-order
rules.description: Ensures that HTML attributes and Angular bindings are sorted based on an expected order

rules.name: banana-in-box
rules.description: Ensures that the two-way data binding syntax is correct

rules.name: button-has-type
rules.description: Ensures that a button has a valid type specified

rules.name: click-events-have-key-events
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that the click event is accompanied with at least one key event keyup, keydown or keypress.

rules.name: conditional-complexity
rules.description: The conditional complexity should not exceed a rational limit

rules.name: cyclomatic-complexity
rules.description: Checks cyclomatic complexity against a specified limit. It is a quantitative measure of the number of linearly independent paths through a program's source code

rules.name: elements-content
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that the heading, anchor and button elements have content in them

rules.name: eqeqeq
rules.description: Requires `===` and `!==` in place of `==` and `!=`

rules.name: i18n
rules.description: Ensures following best practices for i18n.

rules.name: interactive-supports-focus
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that elements with interactive handlers like `(click)` are focusable.

rules.name: label-has-associated-control
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that a label element/component is associated with a form element

rules.name: mouse-events-have-key-events
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that the mouse events \`${MouseEvents.MouseOut}\` and \`${MouseEvents.MouseOver}\` are accompanied by \`${KeyEvents.Focus}\` and \`${KeyEvents.Blur}\` events respectively. Coding for the keyboard is important for users with physical disabilities who cannot use a mouse, AT compatibility, and screenreader users. See more at ${STYLE_GUIDE_LINK}

rules.name: no-any
rules.description: The use of "${ANY_TYPE_CAST_FUNCTION_NAME}" nullifies the compile-time benefits of Angular's type system

rules.name: no-autofocus
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures that the `autofocus` attribute is not used

rules.name: no-call-expression

rules.name: no-distracting-elements
rules.description: [Accessibility] Enforces that no distracting elements are used

rules.name: no-duplicate-attributes
rules.description: Ensures that there are no duplicate input properties or output event listeners

rules.name: no-empty-control-flow
rules.description: Ensures that control flow blocks are not empty. Empty control flow blocks usually occur due to refactoring that wasn't completed. They can cause confusion when reading code.

rules.name: no-inline-styles
rules.description: Disallows the use of inline styles in HTML templates

rules.name: no-interpolation-in-attributes
rules.description: Ensures that property-binding is used instead of interpolation in attributes.

rules.name: no-negated-async
rules.description: Ensures that async pipe results, as well as values used with the async pipe, are not negated

rules.name: no-nested-tags
rules.description:
Denies nesting of `

` and `` tags.

rules.name: no-non-null-assertion
rules.description: Disallows the non-null assertion operator (!) in templates

rules.name: no-positive-tabindex
rules.description: Ensures that the `tabindex` attribute is not positive

rules.name: prefer-at-else

rules.description: Prefer using `@empty` with `@for` loops instead of a separate `@if` or `@else` block to reduce code and make it easier to read.

rules.name: prefer-built-in-pipes
rules.description: Encourages the use of Angular built-in pipes (e.g. lowercase, uppercase, titlecase) instead of certain JavaScript methods in Angular templates.

rules.name: prefer-class-binding
rules.description: Suggests using [class] bindings over ngClass where applicable

rules.name: prefer-contextual-for-variables
rules.description: Ensures that contextual variables are used in @for blocks where possible instead of aliasing them.

rules.name: prefer-control-flow
rules.description: Ensures that the built-in control flow is used.

rules.name: prefer-ngsrc
rules.description: Ensures ngSrc is used instead of src for img elements

rules.name: prefer-self-closing-tags
rules.description: Ensures that self-closing tags are used for elements with a closing tag but no content.

rules.name: prefer-static-string-properties
rules.description: Ensures that static string values use property assignment instead of property binding.

rules.name: prefer-template-literal
rules.description: Ensure that template literals are used instead of concatenating strings or expressions.

rules.name: role-has-required-aria
rules.description: [Accessibility] Ensures elements with ARIA roles have all required properties for that role.

rules.name: table-scope

rules.name: use-track-by-function

rules.name: valid-aria

rules.name: adjacent-overload-signatures

rules.name: array-type

rules.name: await-thenable

rules.description:
Disallow void DOM elements (e.g. ``, `
`) from receiving children

rules.name: component-hook-factories
rules.description: Deprecated: this rule has been removed in 7.1.0.

rules.description: Validates usage of error boundaries instead of try/catch for errors in child components

rules.description: Validates that effect dependencies are exhaustive and without extraneous values

rules.name: fbt
rules.description: Validates usage of fbt

rules.name: gating
rules.description: Validates configuration of [gating mode](https://react.dev/reference/react-compiler/gating)

rules.name: globals
rules.description: Validates against assignment/mutation of globals during render, part of ensuring that [side effects must render outside of render](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#side-effects-must-run-outside-of-render)

rules.name: hooks
rules.description: Validates the rules of hooks

rules.name: immutability
rules.description: Validates against mutating props, state, and other values that [are immutable](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#props-and-state-are-immutable)

rules.name: incompatible-library
rules.description: Validates against usage of libraries which are incompatible with memoization (manual or automatic)

rules.name: invariant
rules.description: Internal invariants

rules.name: memo-dependencies
rules.description: Validates that useMemo() and useCallback() specify comprehensive dependencies without extraneous values. See [`useMemo()` docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/useMemo) for more information.

rules.name: memoized-effect-dependencies
rules.description: Validates that effect dependencies are memoized

rules.name: no-deriving-state-in-effects
rules.description: Validates against deriving values from state in an effect

rules.name: preserve-manual-memoization
rules.description: Validates that existing manual memoized is preserved by the compiler. React Compiler will only compile components and hooks if its inference [matches or exceeds the existing manual memoization](https://react.dev/learn/react-compiler/introduction#what-should-i-do-about-usememo-usecallback-and-reactmemo)

rules.name: purity
rules.description: Validates that [components/hooks are pure](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure) by checking that they do not call known-impure functions

rules.name: refs
rules.description: Validates correct usage of refs, not reading/writing during render. See the "pitfalls" section in [`useRef()` usage](https://react.dev/reference/react/useRef#usage)

rules.name: rule-suppression
rules.description: Validates against suppression of other rules

rules.name: rules-of-hooks
rules.description: enforces the Rules of Hooks

rules.name: set-state-in-effect
rules.description: Validates against calling setState synchronously in an effect. This can indicate non-local derived data, a derived event pattern, or improper external data synchronization.

rules.name: set-state-in-render
rules.description: Validates against setting state during render, which can trigger additional renders and potential infinite render loops

rules.name: static-components
rules.description: Validates that components are static, not recreated every render. Components that are recreated dynamically can reset state and trigger excessive re-rendering

rules.name: syntax
rules.description: Validates against invalid syntax

rules.name: todo
rules.description: Unimplemented features

rules.name: unsupported-syntax
rules.description: Validates against syntax that we do not plan to support in React Compiler

rules.name: use-memo
rules.description: Validates usage of the useMemo() hook against common mistakes. See [`useMemo()` docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/useMemo) for more information.

rules.name: void-use-memo
rules.description: Validates that useMemos always return a value and that the result of the useMemo is used by the component/hook. See [`useMemo()` docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/useMemo) for more information.

rules.description: Disallow empty links

rules.description: Disallow HTML tags

rules.description: Disallow headings without a space after the hash characters

rules.name: no-missing-label-refs
rules.description: Disallow missing label references

rules.name: no-missing-link-fragments
rules.description: Disallow link fragments that do not reference valid headings

rules.name: no-multiple-h1
rules.description: Disallow multiple H1 headings in the same document

rules.name: no-reference-like-urls
rules.description: Disallow URLs that match defined reference identifiers

rules.name: no-reversed-media-syntax
rules.description: Disallow reversed link and image syntax

rules.name: no-space-in-emphasis
rules.description: Disallow spaces around emphasis markers

rules.name: no-unused-definitions
rules.description: Disallow unused definitions

rules.name: require-alt-text
rules.description: Require alternative text for images

rules.name: table-column-count
rules.description: Disallow data rows in a GitHub Flavored Markdown table from having more cells than the header row

rules.name: align-column-definitions
rules.description: Align column definitions vertically inside `CREATE TABLE` so that name, type, and constraints share consistent column offsets

rules.name: align-values
rules.description: Align column values vertically inside multi-row `INSERT ... VALUES (...)` so that each tuple position shares a consistent width across rows

rules.name: consistent-as-for-column-alias
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before column aliases in `SELECT` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-as-for-table-alias
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before table aliases (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-between-over-and
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `x BETWEEN a AND b` vs `x >= a AND x <= b` for closed-interval range checks

rules.name: consistent-create-index-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `CREATE INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-create-or-replace
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CREATE OR REPLACE` for `FUNCTION` / `PROCEDURE` / `VIEW` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-drop-index-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `DROP INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-explicit-inner-join
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `INNER` keyword in `INNER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-explicit-outer-join
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `OUTER` keyword in `LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-fk-not-valid
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `NOT VALID` for `ALTER TABLE ... ADD FOREIGN KEY` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-identity-over-serial
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY` vs `SERIAL` / `BIGSERIAL` / `SMALLSERIAL`

rules.name: consistent-jsonb-over-json
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `jsonb` vs `json` for column types

rules.name: consistent-reindex-concurrently
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `REINDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it)

rules.name: consistent-text-over-varchar
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `text` vs `varchar(n)` for string columns

rules.name: consistent-timestamptz
rules.description: Enforce a consistent stance on `timestamptz` vs `timestamp` (without time zone)

rules.name: no-add-check-constraint-without-not-valid
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK (...)` without `NOT VALID`; the synchronous form holds `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` on the table for the entire validating scan

rules.name: no-add-column-not-null-without-default
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL` without a `DEFAULT` because the migration fails outright on any non-empty table

rules.name: no-add-unique-constraint-directly
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE (...)` written inline; build the index with `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` first, then promote it via `ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE USING INDEX <name>`

rules.name: no-alter-column-type
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE ...` because it can rewrite the table under an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock

rules.name: no-char-type
rules.description: Disallow the blank-padded `char(n)` / `bpchar` column type

rules.name: no-cluster
rules.description: Disallow the `CLUSTER` statement: it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, rewrites the table, and is not maintained afterwards

rules.name: no-composite-primary-key
rules.description: Disallow composite (multi-column) PRIMARY KEY constraints; use a single surrogate key and a UNIQUE constraint on the natural columns instead

rules.name: no-create-role
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE ROLE` / `CREATE USER` in application migrations; manage roles in a separate operator workflow

rules.name: no-cross-join
rules.description: Disallow `CROSS JOIN` (unqualified cartesian product)

rules.name: no-distinct-on-without-order-by
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT DISTINCT ON (...)` without an `ORDER BY`; the surviving row in each group is otherwise non-deterministic

rules.name: no-drop-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN` — every reader of the dropped column breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-drop-database
rules.description: Disallow `DROP DATABASE`; it is catastrophic if run by accident and should not live in versioned SQL

rules.name: no-drop-not-null
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... DROP NOT NULL` — relaxing a NOT NULL constraint surprises every consumer that already assumes the column is non-null

rules.name: no-drop-schema-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE`; it silently removes every object in the schema

rules.name: no-drop-table-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `DROP TABLE ... CASCADE` because it silently removes dependent objects

rules.name: no-equality-with-null
rules.description: Disallow `x = NULL` / `x <> NULL`; PostgreSQL's three-valued logic makes both expressions evaluate to NULL (i.e. neither true nor false), which silently filters away rows the author probably wanted

rules.name: no-grant-all
rules.description: Disallow `GRANT ALL` / `GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES`; enumerate the privileges actually needed so the grant is auditable and a future PG release adding a new privilege does not silently extend it

rules.name: no-grant-to-public
rules.description: Disallow GRANT statements that target the `PUBLIC` pseudo-role

rules.name: no-group-by-ordinal
rules.description: Disallow `GROUP BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or expression instead

rules.name: no-having-without-group-by
rules.description: Disallow `HAVING` without `GROUP BY` — the query aggregates the entire result set, which is almost never the intended shape

rules.name: no-identifier-too-long
rules.description: Disallow identifiers longer than PostgreSQL's `NAMEDATALEN - 1` limit (default 63 bytes)

rules.name: no-implicit-join
rules.description: Disallow comma-separated FROM clauses (implicit cross joins); use explicit JOIN syntax

rules.name: no-leading-wildcard-like
rules.description: Disallow `LIKE`/`ILIKE` patterns that begin with `%` because they cannot use a B-tree index and force a full scan

rules.name: no-money-type
rules.description: Disallow the `money` column type

rules.name: no-natural-join
rules.description: Disallow `NATURAL JOIN`

rules.name: no-not-in-subquery
rules.description: Disallow `NOT IN (subquery)` because NULL values in the subquery silently return zero rows

rules.name: no-numeric-without-precision
rules.description: Require an explicit precision (and scale) on `NUMERIC` / `DECIMAL` columns

rules.name: no-on-delete-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `ON DELETE CASCADE` on foreign keys; cascading deletes are easy to write but can wipe out far more rows than the author intended

rules.name: no-order-by-ordinal
rules.description: Disallow `ORDER BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or alias instead

rules.name: no-rename-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN` — every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-rename-table
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO` — every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time

rules.name: no-rule
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE RULE`; PostgreSQL's rule system is a known foot-gun and is effectively deprecated in favor of triggers and views

rules.name: no-security-definer-without-search-path
rules.description: Disallow `SECURITY DEFINER` functions that do not pin `search_path`; an attacker who controls a schema in the caller's `search_path` can shadow built-in objects and escalate privileges

rules.name: no-select-into
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT ... INTO target FROM ...` (creates a new table); use `CREATE TABLE AS SELECT ...` instead

rules.name: no-select-star
rules.description: Disallow `SELECT *` in queries to keep result schemas stable when the underlying table changes

rules.name: no-set-not-null
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... SET NOT NULL` because it scans the whole table under ACCESS EXCLUSIVE

rules.name: no-set-search-path
rules.description: Disallow `SET search_path = ...` in versioned SQL; qualify identifiers with their schema instead

rules.name: no-syntax-error
rules.description: Disallow PostgreSQL syntax errors

rules.name: no-temporary-table
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` in versioned SQL — temp tables exist for the session only and rarely belong in migration files

rules.name: no-time-type
rules.description: Disallow `TIME` / `TIME WITH TIME ZONE` columns; they rarely model a real-world value correctly

rules.name: no-truncate-cascade
rules.description: Disallow `TRUNCATE ... CASCADE` because it transitively empties referencing tables

rules.name: no-unlogged-table
rules.description: Disallow `CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE` because unlogged tables are truncated on crash and not replicated

rules.name: no-unnecessary-quoted-identifier
rules.description: Disallow unnecessary double-quoting of identifiers (e.g. `"users"` when `users` would mean the same thing)

rules.name: no-update-primary-key
rules.description: Disallow `UPDATE ... SET <pk> = ...` for columns the rule treats as primary keys

rules.name: no-update-without-from-binding
rules.description: Disallow `UPDATE ... FROM other_table` without a `WHERE` clause; without a join condition the FROM table forms a Cartesian product with the target table and updates every row of the target as many times as `other_table` has rows

rules.name: no-vacuum-full
rules.description: Disallow `VACUUM FULL` because it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE and rewrites the entire table

rules.name: no-volatile-default-on-add-column
rules.description: Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT <volatile>()`; volatile defaults force a full table rewrite under `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` because the stable-default short-cut cannot be used

rules.name: no-with-recursive-without-limit
rules.description: Disallow `WITH RECURSIVE` queries that have no `LIMIT` on the outer SELECT, which can run unboundedly if the recursion's termination condition is wrong

rules.name: plpgsql-keyword-case
rules.description: Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL and PL/pgSQL keywords inside PL/pgSQL function bodies

rules.name: prefer-add-constraint-not-valid
rules.description: Prefer `ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID` followed by a separate `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` to avoid an `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` lock on the full table while it is being scanned

rules.name: prefer-bigint-id
rules.description: Prefer `bigint` for primary-key `id` columns; `int` / `smallint` primary keys risk silent overflow on growing tables

rules.name: prefer-cast-operator
rules.description: Enforce a single style for type casts (`x::int` operator form vs `CAST(x AS int)` function form)

rules.name: prefer-coalesce-over-case
rules.description: Prefer `COALESCE(x, y)` over `CASE WHEN x IS NULL THEN y ELSE x END` (and its IS NOT NULL mirror)

rules.name: prefer-current-timestamp-over-now
rules.description: Prefer SQL-standard `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` / `CURRENT_TIME` over PostgreSQL's `now()` and the timezone-naive `LOCALTIMESTAMP` / `LOCALTIME`

rules.name: prefer-exists-over-in-subquery
rules.description: Prefer `EXISTS (...)` over `... IN (subquery)` so NULL handling is unambiguous and the planner can use a semi-join

rules.name: prefer-explicit-null-ordering
rules.description: When `ORDER BY` specifies an explicit direction, require an explicit `NULLS FIRST` / `NULLS LAST` so null ordering is not implicit

rules.name: prefer-in-list-over-or
rules.description: Prefer `x IN (a, b, c)` over a chain of `x = a OR x = b OR x = c`

rules.name: prefer-keyword-case
rules.description: Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL keywords

rules.name: prefer-not-equals-operator
rules.description: Enforce a single style for the not-equal operator (`<>` or `!=`)

rules.name: require-fk-include-columns
rules.description: Require every foreign-key constraint to include a configured set of columns (e.g. `tenant_id` in a multi-tenant database)

rules.name: require-if-exists
rules.description: Require `IF EXISTS` on every `DROP` statement so re-running a migration on a database that already lost the object does not error

rules.name: require-index-on-fk-column
rules.description: Require an index on every foreign-key column; without it a `DELETE` or `UPDATE` on the referenced table sequentially scans the referencing table to enforce the constraint

rules.name: require-limit
rules.description: Require LIMIT clause in SELECT statements

rules.name: require-named-constraint
rules.description: Require an explicit name on table-level CHECK, UNIQUE, FOREIGN KEY, and EXCLUSION constraints

rules.name: require-on-delete-action
rules.description: Require an explicit `ON DELETE` clause on every foreign-key constraint

rules.name: require-primary-key
rules.description: Require every `CREATE TABLE` to declare a primary key

rules.name: require-schema-qualified-table
rules.description: Require `CREATE TABLE` to use a schema-qualified name (e.g. `audit.events`) so the target schema is explicit

rules.name: require-table-columns
rules.description: Require every `CREATE TABLE` to include a configured set of columns (e.g. multi-tenant / audit columns like `tenant_id`, `created_at`, `updated_by`)

rules.name: require-trailing-semicolon
rules.description: Require a trailing `;` at the end of the SQL file

rules.name: require-where-in-delete
rules.description: Require a WHERE clause in DELETE statements

rules.name: require-where-in-update
rules.description: Require a WHERE clause in UPDATE statements

rules.name: snake-case-column-name
rules.description: Require column names to be snake_case

rules.name: snake-case-table-name
rules.description: Require table names to be snake_case
